paragraphs.body: Odd Object Essay Writing in Composition 21 April 2025 My popop sits on the couch of the hotel room, his head is turned towards the window on the left wall watching the sun go down. He is wearing a white cable knit sweater that I have never seen before and it is too big for him. I'm sure there was a time where it fit him perfectly, maybe before his heart surgery. The sleeves are folded over revealing a singular gold bracelet wrapped tightly around his wrist and the neckline gaps so much so that he has to wear a shirt underneath to keep him warm. Where the sweater is most likely supposed to end is at his thighs instead of his hips. It is a perfectly white sweater, not eggshell or cream colored and there are no hints of spaghetti stains or coffee spills. Maybe the sweater is new? Or my momom is just great with stain remover. On his heart there is a red and gold pin with a cross, there is always a symbol of Catholicism with him no matter where he goes.

annotations.text: He is wearing a white cable knit sweater that I have never seen before and it is too big for him. I'm sure there was a time where it fit him perfectly, maybe before his heart surgery.

annotations.context_left: around his wrist and the neckline gaps so much so that he has to wear a shirt underneath to keep

paragraphs.body: When entering my grandparents’ house, there are signs of their catholic faith everywhere. Several crosses can be seen above door frames and in the center of walls in bedrooms, prayer cards and rosaries can be found at the bottom of drawers and tacked onto pinboards. There was a time when my momom went to church every morning and sometimes she would bring me along. Their church had light blue walls and several stained-glass windows as almost every catholic church does, and I would watch the clock counting down the seconds until the service was over. While she does not go everyday anymore, I sometimes catch her reciting a prayer under her breath before we begin to eat at a restaurant, and she has worn the same gold necklace with a cross pendant for as long as I can remember. My popop is almost never home on weekends  Odd Object Essay Writing in Composition 21 April 2025 because he is a deacon and has several funerals and baptisms to attend to. His robes are always pristine and can be found hanging on the back of the door of their guest bedroom.

annotations.comment: your voice and tone throughout this essay is really compelling.

annotations.context_left: When entering my grandparents’ house, there are signs of their catholic faith everywhere.

paragraphs.body: Faith is a complete trust or confidence in someone or something but can also be defined as the strong belief in God or in the doctrines of a religion, based on spiritual apprehension rather than proof. Faith is a divisive word as some people apply it to religion while others apply it to secular beliefs. My grandparents have always associated faith with Catholicism. Their confidence in God and the tellings of the bible are unwavering. This unwavering belief is something I still can’t quite understand even though I grew up with it. As of right now, what I have faith in are the people in my life. I have faith in my mom who has taught me everything. Every single decision she makes is with my sister and I in mind. I have faith in my sister who sometimes identifies things in myself that I could never see on my own. I have faith in my best friends who manage to make hours spent together feel like minutes, and I have faith in my grandparents who know that neither my parents nor my sister and I go to church anymore and have never said a word of disapproval. My faith is in those surrounding me When I was young it was always a fact that my grandparents were religious. There were no discussions of opinions surrounding the church or their faith, it was just something we all accepted about them. The depth of their faith never shocked me until a spring morning when I awoke at their house. My momom wore a nightgown that resembled a green sea glass. We sat crisscrossed applesauce on the plush living room rug as the sun beams streamed through the open windows. My momom pulled out a box full of chess supplies and quietly in her soft-spoken  Odd Object Essay Writing in Composition 21 April 2025 voice explained the directions, making sure to not wake my popop.I watched her hands as she took out the pieces, her veins were so blue and apparent, and her pale skin was so thin. I was confused and asked her why they looked like that. She explained that as you get older your skin loses collagen and becomes thinner and thinner, revealing your veins. “Oh my god” I replied rudely. She then looked at me, her kind eyes made contact with mine as she told me that I should never use god's name in vain and why “god” was an inappropriate thing to say. This shocked me, I had no idea that such a simple phrase I often used was so offensive. It was clear to me then that Catholicism was not just something they used to occupy their time in retirement. It is their faith, what they live by every single day. This expression of faith is something I could not relate to and I did not understand.

annotations.text: My faith is in those surrounding me
annotations.comment: Nice sentence that points directly at the heart of this essay :)

paragraphs.body: As I grow older, it makes more and more sense to me why my grandparents hold their faith so close to them. It is a source of comfort and purpose, a way that they can give back to their community and live by a set of rules and expectations. Seeing my popop in his oversized white sweater reminded me of the purity associated with religion. While that narrative is one I find flawed and unfair, my popop is someone I have always seen as pure. He is kind and affectionate. He teaches middle school aged children on weekdays and makes bracelets for his students on holidays. He recommends books to me because he knows I love to read, and he brings me rings from his favorite thrift stores. Sometimes, he even ships them to me now that I am at school. He is one of those people that smiles with his eyes and has a very distinct laugh. When he looked over from the hotel window, he reached over with the slouched sleeves of his sweater and gave me a hug.

annotations.text: Seeing my popop in his oversized white sweater reminded me of the purity associated with religion. While that narrative is one I find flawed and unfair, my popop is someone I have always seen as pure.
annotations.comment: beautiful return to your introduction. Great metaphor

paragraphs.body: My version of faith may not be the same as my grandparents, and it is something we don’t usually discuss, but I know that religion is something they inherited from their parents and  Odd Object Essay Writing in Composition 21 April 2025 grandparents. It is comforting in a way to know that they have been devoted to their belief system for so long and I have come to simply associate certain religious things with them, people who I love dearly.

annotations.text: It is comforting in a way to know that they have been devoted to their belief system for so long and I have come to simply associate certain religious things with them, people who I love dearly.
